intitule: Good Governance and Citizenship: Vocabulary Matching

enonce.texte: Match the governance and citizenship vocabulary terms (1-8) with their correct definitions (A-H).

questions.texte: Election
Transparency
Democracy
Candidate
Polling station
Vote
Governance
Citizen

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the appropriate word from the box: election, transparency, democracy, candidate, vote, citizen, governance, polling station.

questions.texte: A ________ is a person who has the right to participate in the political life of a country.
The process of choosing a leader through voting is called an ________.
A ________ is a person who competes for a political position.
Good ________ requires honesty and accountability from leaders.
________ means that the government's actions are open and clear to the people.
In a ________, citizens have the power to choose their leaders.
Citizens go to the ________ to cast their ballots.
Every adult citizen has the right to ________ in a democratic election.

intitule: Applying Governance Vocabulary in Real-Life Contexts

enonce.texte: Read the following scenarios and use the appropriate governance and citizenship vocabulary to answer the questions.

questions.texte: In a village in Cameroon, the population is preparing to choose their new chief. What is this process called?
questions.reponse: This process is called an election.

questions.texte: A citizen wants to know how the village funds are being spent. What principle of governance is he/she expecting?
A young man wishes to stand for the position of school representative. What title does he have?
A citizen is going to the local primary school to choose their representative. Where is he/she going?
questions.reponse: He/she is going to a polling station.

questions.texte: A political system where citizens have the power to choose their leaders. What is this system called?

intitule: Analyzing the Importance of Citizenship and Good Governance in Cameroon

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions that follow using your knowledge of governance and citizenship vocabulary.

"In Cameroon, good governance is essential for the development of the country. Citizens need to participate actively in the democratic process. However, many young people do not understand the importance of voting. The government must ensure transparency and fairness during elections to maintain the trust of the population. Local authorities also play a key role in promoting citizenship and encouraging people to take part in community development."

questions.texte: According to the text, what is essential for the development of Cameroon?
questions.reponse: According to the text, good governance is essential for the development of Cameroon.

questions.texte: What must the government ensure during elections to maintain the trust of the population?
questions.reponse: The government must ensure transparency and fairness during elections.

questions.texte: Why should young people participate actively in the democratic process?
questions.reponse: Young people should participate actively in the democratic process because it is their civic duty and it helps shape the future of the country.

questions.texte: What role do local authorities play in promoting citizenship?
questions.reponse: Local authorities play a key role in encouraging people to take part in community development and in promoting citizenship.

intitule: Mobilizing Citizens: A Speech on Good Governance

enonce.texte: Imagine you are a community leader in a village in Cameroon. You have been asked to give a speech to encourage citizens to vote in the upcoming municipal elections. Write a short speech (5-7 sentences) using at least five of the following vocabulary words: election, democracy, vote, candidate, transparency, citizen, governance, polling station.

questions.texte: Write your speech here.
questions.reponse: Good morning, fellow citizens. As we approach the upcoming municipal elections, I call on every citizen to exercise their right to vote. Our democracy depends on the active participation of every citizen. Choose your candidate wisely, and insist on transparency in the electoral process. Let us go to our polling stations on election day to make our voices heard. Good governance starts with our vote. Thank you.

enonce.texte:
Read the following statement and evaluate it based on the principles of good governance and citizenship. Then, answer the questions that follow.

"In a recent election, a candidate promised to improve the school infrastructure in a village. After being elected, he fulfilled his promise and built a new library for the students."

questions.texte: What principle of good governance is illustrated in this statement? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: The principle of accountability is illustrated. The candidate made a promise and fulfilled it after being elected, showing that he is accountable to the citizens.

questions.texte: How does this situation encourage citizens to participate in future elections?
questions.reponse: This situation encourages citizens to participate because they can see that their votes have a real impact. When they see leaders fulfilling their promises, they are more likely to trust the electoral system and vote again.
